Question (4)
1] We mean that the JAVA is not restricted by a
specific system architecture .
2] They introduced Applets which extend the
functionality of the web, and also servlet that extended
functionality of the server.
3] The OOP treats the code lines as instructions
and emphasizes on the procedure, on the other hand
OOP encapsulate the code and data is manipulates and
emphasized on data.
4] JRE or JAVA Runtime Environment consists of library
and JVM to Run the code.
5] Inheritance: new classes inherits the attributes and
methods of older classes. Polymorphism: Allow one
interface to be used for general use case, Inheritance keep
the old classes methods while polymorphism might alter them
6] Encapsulations is the mechanism that binds the code
and data is manipulates, and Keep them safe from
outside interference and misuse.
7] Class abstracts and interfaces.
8] 1) finished and completed the task normally.
2) terminated due to unusual events.

3) Stopped due to runtime error
9) System.In System.out System.err
10) JAVA swing was built on top of AWT
Question 2
1) The C++ language was called "C with classes"
True
2) It is not possible for an interface to inherit another interfaces
properties
True
3) The Java byte code is a .class file that is executed
by the Java run-time system
True
4) Run time polymorphism could be achieved by method
Overloading
False, it is achieved by Overriding
5) Abstract classes could have non-abstract methods within them
False, that would lead to the diamond problem,
which causes the class to exted extend till [UNK] errors out
6) JAVA threads are light weigh threads
True

7] There is one JDBC driver for all database sources
⊗ False each source has its own implementations for
JDBC driver - True
1] Enhancement is one of the reason that computer
language innovation occurred
2] C is an example of a POP language
instructions
3] POP does not allow [UNK] flow freely in the
Program
4] Applets are small programs that need a Java
enabled web browser to be executed
5] a frame is a component which can contain other
elements like buttons
6] The model-view controller (MVC) architecture is used in Swing.
7] JDBC is a Java API to connect and interact with
databases
